summary: Tested ATS Resume Tailor full-stack app (backend + frontend). All 5 backend pytest tests pass (health flags, 422 validation, fail-fast Overleaf 500, root). All 9 frontend flows pass: nav loads, hero headline 'Beat the ATS. / Land the interview.' renders, 3 how-it-works cards, two textareas side-by-side on desktop, generate button correctly disabled until both textareas filled, hero & nav CTAs scroll to generator, submit triggers POST /api/generate which returns 500 with 'Overleaf credentials not configured...' message, error card displays the message, retry button hides error card, footer present. No unexpected console errors (only the expected 500 from the intentionally unconfigured Overleaf creds).

minor.issue: On generic exception fallback, returns 200 with status='error' in body instead of HTTP 4xx/5xx (lines 606-608). Frontend still handles via data.message, but a non-2xx status would be more REST-correct.

critical_code_review_comments: server.py line 606-608: catch-all returns GenerateResponse(status='error') with HTTP 200; consider raising HTTPException(500) instead so frontend axios .catch path is exercised consistently.
Rate limiter is in-memory (defaultdict). Will reset on every backend restart and is not multi-worker safe. Acceptable for MVP single-process supervisor setup; document this.
_check_rate_limit appends to bucket BEFORE Overleaf-creds fail-fast check, so failed-fast 500 calls still consume a rate-limit slot. Minor UX concern.
11th-request 429 rate-limit test was not executed in this session to avoid burning the bucket; logic looks correct on inspection (deque + 3600s window, _RATE_LIMIT_MAX=10).

LATEX_SYSTEM_PROMPT contains several unbalanced braces in the literal template (e.g., \newcommand{\resumeItem}[1]{\item\small{#1 \vspace{-1pt}} on line 285, similar on 295, 302, 305, 307). These are part of the system prompt sent to Claude, not executed locally, so they don't break the backend startup, but Claude is being asked to 'keep skeleton intact' - the broken skeleton may produce LaTeX that fails to compile on Overleaf. Recommend reviewing the template once Overleaf creds are wired up.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Overleaf cookies are intentionally empty in /app/backend/.env so the happy path cannot be tested. Once OVERLEAF_SESSION_COOKIE and OVERLEAF_GCLB_TOKEN are populated, retest the full PDF pipeline end-to-end (expect ~30-60s response time due to 2 Claude calls + Overleaf compile). LATEX_SYSTEM_PROMPT template skeleton has potential brace imbalances worth re-verifying with a real compile.
